subject: [PATCH 0/3] mm: handle device-private PMDs in walk callbacks

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Reviewer suggested adding Balbir to the CC list for the patch series

Reviewer expressed dissatisfaction with 'existing problem' reports being included in Usama's patch series, suggesting they should be a passive reporting mode for sashiko and not added to other series.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed concern about potential issues with device-private PMDs, suggesting a thorough audit of all affected code paths to proactively identify and address any potential problems.
reviews.raw_body:
+cc Balbir

Thanks for the series!

I think Balbir should be cc'd no?

On Tue, Jul 07, 2026 at 06:45:06AM -0700, Usama Arif wrote:

---

Yeah I don't love these 'existing problem' reports (distracting from other work,
add workload, really it feels like that should be a passive reporting mode for
sashiko not arbitrarily added to other series), but sending it as a separate
series is indeed the right way :)

These of course should never block an unrelated series.

---

Hmm we've had a whole host of issues with device-private PMDs recently.

Balbir - would it be possible to audit all of the code paths and proactively see
if there's anything else that could hit problems here?

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about get_maintainers.pl not including authors of commits being fixed and suggested modifying the script to include them
reviews.raw_body:
Ah yes, sorry about that! I just sent the patches to the list I got from
get_maintainers.pl.

I think maybe we should modify get_maintainers.pl so that if there is
a "Fixes" in commit message, than the author of that commit is also
in the output from that script?

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Zi Yan noted that the patch does not handle the case where a PMD is being migrated and its device-private swap entry is still present, which can cause issues when walking callbacks are called on such PMDs.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a PMD is being migrated to device memory and its swap entry has been removed, which can lead to a vmemmap dereference.
reviews.raw_body:
+Alistair as well.

On 7 Jul 2026, at 10:01, Lorenzo Stoakes wrote:

---

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested adding a !pmd_present() check in patch 1/3, similar to patches 2/3 and 3/3, to make the code more robust against future cases.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Usama,

Thank you for the fixes! I think all of them look good and they are fixes
so please feel free to add my

Reviewed-by: Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com>

One thing I wanted to maybe consider is for 1/3, whether it could be
good to gate by !pmd_present() like the sites in 2/3 and 3/3, maybe
that will be more robust in the future if there are other cases we
should protect against (and the same as queue_folios_pte_range, which
uses the !pte_present() check too).

For 2/3 and 3/3 we already have the !pmd_present() check, would it be
worth considering just changing the VM_BUG_ON condition to include
!pmd_is_device_private_entry()?

Just wanted to toss my 2c. Thanks again for the fixes Usama!
Joshua

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer Zi Yan suggested that the patch should also increment qp->nr_failed when handling device-private PMDs and pointed out that other softleaf entries should be excluded due to pmd_is_huge() returning true for non-present, non-none PMDs.
reviews.raw_body:
Should it also increase qp->nr_failed like pmd migration entry above?
It also applies to your "if (unlikely(pmd_is_swap_entry(*pmd)))" in the
PMD-level swap patchset.

In addition, since pmd_is_huge() returns true for all non-present
non-none pmds, other softleaf entries should also be excluded here? Oh,
because there is no PMD level hwpoison or pmd marker.

-- 
Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is generally good but suggested adding a CC to the stable kernel mailing list to ensure it gets backported if necessary.
sentiment_signals: Requested additional consideration for stable kernel
reviews.raw_body:
LGTM.

Reviewed-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>

Since it could trigger VM_BUG_ON(), probably cc stable?


-- 
Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch series addresses issues caused by device-private PMDs in walk callbacks. Device-private PMDs can be created when an HMM-based GPU driver migrates anonymous THPs to device memory, leading to unexpected behavior and potential crashes. The patches modify various MM walk callbacks to skip or handle these device-private PMDs correctly, preventing VM_BUG_ONs, oops, and incorrect LRU isolation.

reviews.author: Balbir Singh
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Balbir Singh expressed appreciation for explicit CCs in the patch series

Reviewer Balbir Singh noted that his own use case did not expose the issue, but acknowledged that there are multiple reports of problems caused by device-private PMDs being reachable in MM walk callbacks.

reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a PMD is being migrated to device memory, which can lead to incorrect behavior in MM walk callbacks
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks, my linux-mm monitoring has been largely skimming through the
list. Explict cc's are always appreciated.

---

Let me run through this and audit them. My usage of device private PMD
did not expose them, but I can see a bunch of reports in the link above

---

Thanks Lorenzo!

Balbir

reviews.author: Balbir Singh
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested splitting the current patch into two separate patches, one for making madvise pmd free work for THP PMD entries and another for the current changes.
reviews.raw_body:
The change makes sense, I spent some time thinking if we should make
madvise pmd free work for THP PMD entries, but that should be a separate
patchset, it is much more complex and involved.

Reviewed-by: Balbir Singh <balbirs@nvidia.com>

reviews.author: Balbir Singh
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Balbir Singh noted that the patch description implies skipping device-private PMDs via !pmd_present(), but questioned whether this is sufficient and suggested setting qp->nr_failed++ for these entries, similar to migration entries.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a PMD is being migrated to device memory, and suggested adding a check for this condition in pmd_trans_huge_lock()
sentiment_signals: requested clarification on skipping device-private PMDs
suggested modification to handling of device-private PMDs
reviews.raw_body:
The changelog lead me to believe that this is skipped via
!pmd_present()? Do we need to skip just device private entries Shouldn't
we set qp->nr_failed++ similar to what we do for migration entries or do
we silently ignore them?

---

Thanks,
Balbir

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch series addresses issues caused by device-private PMDs in walk callbacks. Device-private PMDs can be created when an HMM-based GPU driver migrates anonymous THPs to device memory, leading to unexpected behavior and potential crashes. The patches modify various MM walk callbacks to skip or handle these device-private PMDs correctly, preventing VM_BUG_ONs, oops, and incorrect LRU isolation.
